claim: Hand A's data satisfies three external structural predictions made independently by prior researchers in the Voynich literature: (a) Stolfi's crust-only fraction ~75%; (b) Stolfi's prefix/suffix dealer asymmetry ~0.14 vs ~0.80; (c) Caveney's word-final n frequency >=85%. If Brain-V's Hand A characterisation is correct, all three should pass. The number of passing predictions out of three quantifies external convergence between Brain-V's autonomous findings and 25 years of prior Voynich structural analysis.
evidence_for: Stolfi's crust-mantle-core grammar (2000, voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html) is the best-established Voynich word-structure model; its crust-only fraction (~75%) and prefix/suffix asymmetry (0.14 vs 0.80) are derived primarily from Currier B but stated as applying to the whole manuscript.

Caveney (2020, voynich.ninja thread 3354) made a specific, testable positional claim — word-final n >=90% — as part of a Basque-leaning proposal. This is the only pre-Brain-V Basque-language-adjacent proposal with a quantitative structural prediction concrete enough to pre-register against.

Brain-V's Basque-leaning Hand A findings emerged independently, without reference to Stolfi or Caveney. If the findings are structurally valid, external convergence is expected; if not, at least one of the three tests should fail.
All three predictions pre-date Brain-V (Stolfi by ~25 years, Caveney by ~5 years). They are therefore unbiased external benchmarks — pass/fail cannot be influenced by Brain-V's internal framing.
evidence_against: Stolfi derived his numbers primarily from Currier B; Hand A may have systematically different crust/mantle/core ratios even if the layer paradigm holds. The 75% crust-only figure may be a B-specific statistic mis-applied to A.

Caveney's word-final n figure (>=90%) was stated as an impression on a forum, not derived from a rigorous corpus measurement. The prediction itself may be imprecise. Pass band was loosened to [0.85, 1.00] accordingly.

Stolfi's prefix/suffix dealer counts (0.14 vs 0.80) require an operational definition of 'productive stem' and 'dealer'. Brain-V's definition may not match Stolfi's exactly, introducing measurement bias that could cause a pass or fail for reasons other than the underlying claim.

Convergence does not imply correctness: three prior researchers could all be reflecting the same systematic artefact in the EVA transliteration rather than a genuine linguistic structure. A pass on all three is compatible with Brain-V and the prior researchers all being wrong in the same direction.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Hand A corpus, EVA transliteration. Use the same folio partition as H-BV-BASQUE-* series. Hand A tokens only; Hand B excluded from all three tests.

  TEST 1 — STOLFI CRUST-ONLY FRACTION:
    Stolfi layer categories (LOCKED per Stolfi 2000, voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html):
      CORE:   {t, p, k, f, cth, cph, ckh, cfh}
      MANTLE: {ch, sh, ee}
      CRUST:  {d, l, r, s, n, x, i, m, g, q, o, y, a, e}
    Tokenise each Hand A word into Stolfi units. A token is 'crust-only' if it contains zero core glyphs and zero mantle ligatures. Compute:
      crust_only_fraction = |{tokens with no core and no mantle}| / |all Hand A tokens|
    PASS if crust_only_fraction in [0.70, 0.80].
    FAIL otherwise.

  TEST 2 — STOLFI PREFIX/SUFFIX DEALER ASYMMETRY:
    For each Hand A token with at least one core glyph, split the token at the first core glyph into (prefix, core, suffix). Stolfi's 'dealer' count per position = mean number of crust glyphs in that position across all split tokens.
    Compute:
      prefix_dealer_mean = mean number of crust glyphs in prefix across all split tokens
      suffix_dealer_mean = mean number of crust glyphs in suffix across all split tokens
      asymmetry_ratio    = prefix_dealer_mean / suffix_dealer_mean
    Stolfi's published values (without q): prefix ~0.14, suffix ~0.80, ratio ~0.175.
    PASS if asymmetry_ratio < 0.25.
    FAIL if asymmetry_ratio >= 0.25.
    NOTE: exclude q-initial tokens from this test per Stolfi's original specification.

  TEST 3 — CAVENEY WORD-FINAL n FREQUENCY:
    For each Hand A token, check whether the final glyph (after EVA tokenisation) is 'n'.
    Compute:
      final_n_fraction = |{Hand A tokens ending in 'n'}| / |all Hand A tokens|
    PASS if final_n_fraction in [0.85, 1.00].
    FAIL otherwise.
    NOTE: Caveney's original claim was 'word-final or coda position >=90%'. Because 'coda' is ambiguous in EVA, the locked test uses strict word-final only. Pass band loosened to 0.85 to absorb the coda/final-only distinction.

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    STRONG CONVERGENCE: 3/3 predictions pass. Brain-V's Hand A characterisation aligns with three independent prior frameworks. The composite-outer hypothesis (test (3) of the v2 plan) becomes a refinement of an already-validated structural picture.
    MODERATE CONVERGENCE: 2/3 pass. Identify which specific prediction fails. Document what the failure implies about Brain-V's framework. Composite-outer hypothesis still worth running but with explicit acknowledgment of the divergence.
    WEAK/NO CONVERGENCE: 0-1/3 pass. External predictions disagree with Brain-V's data. Either the prior researchers were wrong, or Brain-V has a systematic measurement error. v2 must address this explicitly — either by identifying a defensible reason the priors don't apply to Hand A, or by re-examining Brain-V's tokenisation/statistical pipeline.

  REPORTING:
    For each test, record: the raw statistic, the pass band, the pass/fail verdict. Do not retrospectively adjust pass bands — they are locked by this file.
test_threshold: STRONG if 3/3. MODERATE if 2/3. WEAK if 0-1/3.

test_1_stolfi_crust_only.interpretation: Hand A is roughly half-mantle-bearing. Stolfi's 'crust-only' (no core, no mantle) applies to 27% of Hand A tokens, far from his ~75%. Even relaxing to 'no core allowing mantle' only gets to 53%. Hand A has substantially more mantle and core content than Stolfi's B-derived baseline.

test_2_stolfi_asymmetry.interpretation: Asymmetry DIRECTION is confirmed (suffix 3.4x prefix, same direction as Stolfi's 5.7x). Asymmetry RATIO narrowly misses the 0.25 threshold (0.29). Absolute dealer counts are roughly 5x Stolfi's values (0.70/2.42 vs 0.14/0.80), indicating Hand A has substantially denser crust glyphs per position than Stolfi's B-derived corpus. The structural pattern Stolfi identified is qualitatively present; the specific numeric threshold fails.

interpretation_amendment_2026_04_17.triggered_by: H-BV-DENSITY-DIAGNOSIS-01 verdict OPERATIONALISATION_DRIFT (candidate b passes at fraction_methodological = 1.06)
interpretation_amendment_2026_04_17.revision: The interpretation above — that Hand A has substantially denser crust glyphs per position than Stolfi's baseline — is not supported under scrutiny. DENSITY-DIAGNOSIS-01 ran Brain-V's Stolfi operationalisation on Hand B directly and measured suffix_dealer_mean(B) = 2.5168, nearly identical to Hand A's 2.4182. Both hands are ~3x Stolfi's published 0.80. The 3x gap is not a Hand-A-specific property of the manuscript; it is a drift between Brain-V's implementation and Stolfi's published numbers, affecting both hands equally.
interpretation_amendment_2026_04_17.locked_result_unchanged: The T2 pass/fail verdict (FAIL), raw statistics (prefix 0.7042, suffix 2.4182, ratio 0.2912), and pass band (< 0.25) are UNCHANGED. Pre-registration discipline: published verdicts stand.
interpretation_amendment_2026_04_17.what_still_holds: The asymmetry DIRECTION finding (suffix >> prefix in Hand A) is still valid — it is a within-hand positional finding and does not depend on the Stolfi baseline. The Stolfi-grammar qualitative structure is still confirmed in Hand A.
interpretation_amendment_2026_04_17.what_does_not_hold: Any claim that Hand A is 'unusually dense' relative to Currier B, or that Hand A has 'excess suffix dealers', or that the 2.42 value indicates Hand-A-specific morphology richness. Those readings were artefacts of comparing Brain-V's pipeline to Stolfi's published numbers without a prerequisite sanity check.

test_3_caveney_final_n.locked_metric_definition: tokens ending in n / all Hand A tokens
test_3_caveney_final_n.specification_error_note: The locked metric inverts Caveney's actual claim. Caveney (2020) asserted 'of all n occurrences, almost all are word-final or coda position'. The pre-registered metric computed '(tokens ending in n) / (all tokens)' — this measures how common final-n is in the vocabulary, not whether n is word-final when it appears. The correct Caveney-spec metric is (n occurrences word-final) / (all n occurrences). Diagnostic result for the correct metric below.

diagnostic_caveney_original_metric.description: of all n occurrences in Hand A, fraction that are word-final

test_3_caveney_final_n.interpretation: The locked test fails under the pre-registered (erroneously specified) metric. Caveney's ACTUAL claim, run as a diagnostic, yields 94.6% — firmly in the [0.85, 1.00] band. When n appears in Hand A, it is almost always word-final. This is a methodology lesson (pre-reg the exact computation, not just the natural-language claim) rather than a Brain-V or Caveney error.

tests_run.verdict_note: 0/3 passes under the locked pass bands. Per the decision rule, this is WEAK_CONVERGENCE. The locked verdict stands. However, the three failures have qualitatively different character: T1 reflects a genuine quantitative disagreement with Stolfi (Hand A differs materially from the Currier-B-derived baseline); T2 narrowly fails the ratio threshold while confirming Stolfi's qualitative direction with ~5x denser absolute counts; T3 fails due to a specification error in the pre-registration (inverted denominator) — the underlying Caveney claim is confirmed at 94.6% under its correct metric. Raw verdict: WEAK. Honest read: one genuine divergence (T1), one partial convergence (T2), one methodology error (T3).

verdict_note: WEAK_CONVERGENCE (0/3 under locked bands). Each failure has a distinct character: T1 Stolfi crust-only — genuine quantitative divergence (Hand A 27% vs Stolfi's ~75%); Hand A has substantially more mantle content than Stolfi's Currier-B-derived baseline. T2 Stolfi asymmetry — qualitative confirmation (suffix 3.4x prefix) but narrow quantitative miss on ratio (0.29 vs <0.25); absolute dealer counts ~5x Stolfi's numbers. T3 Caveney final-n — specification error in pre-registration (inverted denominator); Caveney's actual claim confirmed at 94.6% under its correct metric, but the locked metric fails at 15.5%.
findings_for_followup: T1 is the most substantive finding: Hand A genuinely differs from Stolfi's Currier-B baseline on the crust-only fraction. If Brain-V's Hand A characterisation is correct, the composite-outer hypothesis (3) must NOT assume Stolfi's ~75% crust-only figure; it is a B-specific baseline. The composite-outer test design should compute Hand-A-specific crust/mantle/core fractions rather than importing Stolfi's B numbers.

T2's narrow miss (0.29 vs <0.25) plus 5x scale difference suggests Hand A has denser per-position crust loading than B. Under an agglutinative reading, this could reflect more case/derivational suffixes per stem in A than in B. The composite-outer test (3) should investigate whether the excess crust glyphs on the suffix side are consistent with Basque-style morphological stacking, or whether they indicate a different structural regime entirely.

T3's specification error is a methodology lesson for the entire pre-registration workflow: pre-register the EXACT numerator and denominator of each metric, not just the natural-language claim. Caveney's claim was 'of n occurrences, most are word-final' — a positional-within-glyph-occurrences measure. The pre-reg converted this to 'fraction of tokens ending in n' — a vocabulary-level measure. Future pre-registrations should include a worked mini-example showing the denominator.

The 94.6% Caveney-correct result is a LOAD-BEARING finding for the composite-outer test (3). It confirms that n is strongly word-final-biased in Hand A — making n a prime candidate for an outer-slot morpheme (case marker, verbal ending, or equivalent). The composite-outer hypothesis should include n as one of the candidate outer morphemes to decompose against.

Stolfi's qualitative structural picture (suffix-heavy asymmetry) holds; his quantitative baselines (75% crust-only, 0.14/0.80 dealers) do not transfer to Hand A. For v2's prior-work section: cite Stolfi for the qualitative grammar, not for specific numeric thresholds. Hand A requires its own derived baselines.

The 0/3 locked verdict underscores that external predictions from prior work cannot be naively transplanted onto Brain-V's Hand-A-specific framework. The composite-outer test (3) should be pre-registered with Hand-A-derived baselines (measured from Brain-V's own corpus) rather than imported from Stolfi's published values. This does not weaken the prior-work citation — it clarifies scope.

methodology_note.issue: The pre-registered metric inverted the denominator of Caveney's actual claim. Caveney (2020) claimed 'of all n occurrences in the corpus, almost all are word-final or coda'. This is a positional-within-occurrences measure: numerator = n occurrences that are word-final; denominator = all n occurrences. The locked pre-registration instead defined numerator = tokens ending in n; denominator = all Hand A tokens. That is a vocabulary-prevalence measure, not a positional measure.
methodology_note.impact: Under the locked (incorrect) metric: 15.46% FAIL. Under Caveney's actual metric (recorded as diagnostic): 94.61% would PASS the same [0.85, 1.00] band. The divergence is large — from a 0.15 'refute' to a 0.95 'confirm' — and reverses the interpretation of one of three locked tests.
methodology_note.discipline_decision: The locked verdict (0/3 WEAK_CONVERGENCE) stands as recorded. Pre-registration integrity requires that results report against the locked specification, not against what the specification ought to have said. Post-hoc re-scoring against a 'correct' metric would defeat the purpose of pre-registration.
methodology_note.corrective_action: A new, separately pre-registered hypothesis H-BV-CAVENEY-FINAL-N-01 carries Caveney's actual claim under a precisely operationalised metric with worked denominator example. That hypothesis — not this one — is the authoritative Brain-V test of Caveney's positional constraint going forward.
methodology_note.generalised_lesson: Future pre-registrations must (a) specify numerator and denominator explicitly; (b) include at least one worked mini-example showing the computation on a concrete token; (c) distinguish 'fraction of tokens with property X' from 'fraction of occurrences at position Y' — these are distinct measures and natural-language claims often leave this ambiguous.
methodology_note.amendment_status: This note was added as an amendment on 2026-04-17 after execution. It does not alter the locked test, locked bands, locked verdict, or recorded raw statistics. It adds an audit trail for the specification error.

reasoning: Prior-work literature search (2026-04-17, saved to outputs/reports/prior-work-basque-voynich.md) established that Brain-V's Basque-leaning Hand A findings emerged independently of the published Voynich structural literature. Three external predictions are concrete enough to pre-register against: two from Stolfi (2000), one from Caveney (2020). Havrankova (2025) was considered but contains zero quantitative predictions, so cannot be locked against. Testing Brain-V's Hand A data against these three external predictions provides an unbiased convergence check before designing the composite-outer hypothesis test (v2 test 3). A strong external convergence result would let the composite-outer test be pre-registered as a refinement of an already-validated picture; a weak convergence would force a re-examination of Brain-V's tokenisation pipeline before further Basque-specific testing.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 pre-registration derived from prior-work search findings (outputs/reports/prior-work-basque-voynich.md). External predictions sourced from: Stolfi 2000 (voynich.nu/hist/stolfi/grammar.html) and Caveney 2020 (voynich.ninja thread 3354).
